english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Ephesians

verses.latin_vulgate: Obsecro itaque vos ego vinctus in Domino, ut digne ambuletis vocatione, qua vocati estis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ οὖν ὑμᾶς ἐγὼ ὁ δέσμιος ἐν κυρίῳ ἀξίως περιπατῆσαι τῆς κλήσεως ἡς ἐκλήθητε
verses.king_james: I therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you that ye walk worthy of the vocation wherewith ye are called,

verses.latin_vulgate: cum omni humilitate, et mansuetudine, cum patientia, supportantes invicem in caritate,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ πάσης ταπεινοφροσύνης καὶ πρᾳότητος μετὰ μακροθυμίας ἀνεχόμενοι ἀλλήλων ἐν ἀγάπῃ
verses.king_james: With all lowliness and meekness, with longsuffering, forbearing one another in love;

verses.latin_vulgate: solliciti servare unitatem Spiritus in vinculo pacis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σπουδάζοντες τηρεῖν τὴν ἑνότητα τοῦ πνεύματος ἐν τῷ συνδέσμῳ τῆς εἰρήνης
verses.king_james: Endeavouring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unum corpus, et unus Spiritus, sicut vocati estis in una spe vocationis vestræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἓν σῶμα καὶ ἓν πνεῦμα καθὼς καὶ ἐκλήθητε ἐν μιᾷ ἐλπίδι τῆς κλήσεως ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: *There is* one body, and one Spirit, even as ye are called in one hope of your calling;

verses.latin_vulgate: Unus Dominus, una fides, unum baptisma.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἷς κύριος μία πίστις ἓν βάπτισμα
verses.king_james: One Lord, one faith, one baptism,

verses.latin_vulgate: Unus Deus et Pater omnium, qui est super omnes, et per omnia, et in omnibus nobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἷς θεὸς καὶ πατὴρ πάντων ὁ ἐπὶ πάντων καὶ διὰ πάντων καὶ ἐν πᾶσιν ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: One God and Father of all, who *is* above all, and through all, and in you all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unicuique autem nostrum data est gratia secundum mensuram donationis Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἑνὶ δὲ ἑκάστῳ ἡμῶν ἐδόθη ἡ χάρις κατὰ τὸ μέτρον τῆς δωρεᾶς τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: But unto every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod dicit: Ascendens in altum, captivam duxit captivitatem: dedit dona hominibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ λέγει ἀναβὰς εἰς ὕψος ᾐχμαλώτευσεν αἰχμαλωσίαν καὶ ἔδωκεν δόματα τοῖς ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: Wherefore he saith, When he ascended up on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts unto men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod autem ascendit, quid est, nisi quia et descendit primum in inferiores partes terræ?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸ δὲ ἀνέβη τί ἐστιν εἰ μὴ ὅτι καὶ κατέβη πρῶτον εἰς τὰ κατώτερα μέρη τῆς γῆς
verses.king_james: (Now that he ascended, what is it but that he also descended first into the lower parts of the earth?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui descendit, ipse est et qui ascendit super omnes cælos, ut impleret omnia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ καταβὰς αὐτός ἐστιν καὶ ὁ ἀναβὰς ὑπεράνω πάντων τῶν οὐρανῶν ἵνα πληρώσῃ τὰ πάντα
verses.king_james: He that descended is the same also that ascended up far above all heavens, that he might fill all things.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipse dedit quosdam quidem apostolos, quosdam autem prophetas, alios vero evangelistas, alios autem pastores et doctores,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ αὐτὸς ἔδωκεν τοὺς μὲν ἀποστόλους τοὺς δὲ προφήτας τοὺς δὲ εὐαγγελιστάς τοὺς δὲ ποιμένας καὶ διδασκάλους
verses.king_james: And he gave some, apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, pastors and teachers;

verses.latin_vulgate: ad consummationem sanctorum in opus ministerii, in ædificationem corporis Christi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πρὸς τὸν καταρτισμὸν τῶν ἁγίων εἰς ἔργον διακονίας εἰς οἰκοδομὴν τοῦ σώματος τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: For the perfecting of the saints, for the work of the ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ:

verses.latin_vulgate: donec occurramus omnes in unitatem fidei, et agnitionis Filii Dei, in virum perfectum, in mensuram ætatis plenitudinis Christi:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μέχρι καταντήσωμεν οἱ πάντες εἰς τὴν ἑνότητα τῆς πίστεως καὶ τῆς ἐπιγνώσεως τοῦ υἱοῦ τοῦ θεοῦ εἰς ἄνδρα τέλειον εἰς μέτρον ἡλικίας τοῦ πληρώματος τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut jam non simus parvuli fluctuantes, et circumferamur omni vento doctrinæ in nequitia hominum, in astutia ad circumventionem erroris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα μηκέτι ὦμεν νήπιοι κλυδωνιζόμενοι καὶ περιφερόμενοι παντὶ ἀνέμῳ τῆς διδασκαλίας ἐν τῇ κυβείᾳ τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἐν πανουργίᾳ πρὸς τὴν μεθοδείαν τῆς πλάνης
verses.king_james: That we *henceforth* be no more children, tossed to and fro, and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the sleight of men, *and* cunning craftiness, whereby they lie in wait to deceive;

verses.latin_vulgate: Veritatem autem facientes in caritate, crescamus in illo per omnia, qui est caput Christus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀληθεύοντες δὲ ἐν ἀγάπῃ αὐξήσωμεν εἰς αὐτὸν τὰ πάντα ὅς ἐστιν ἡ κεφαλή ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: But speaking the truth in love, may grow up into him in all things, which is the head, *even* Christ:

verses.latin_vulgate: ex quo totum corpus compactum et connexum per omnem juncturam subministrationis, secundum operationem in mensuram uniuscujusque membri, augmentum corporis facit in ædificationem sui in caritate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξ οὗ πᾶν τὸ σῶμα συναρμολογούμενον καὶ συμβιβαζόμενον διὰ πάσης ἁφῆς τῆς ἐπιχορηγίας κατ᾽ ἐνέργειαν ἐν μέτρῳ ἑνὸς ἑκάστου μέρους τὴν αὔξησιν τοῦ σώματος ποιεῖται εἰς οἰκοδομὴν ἑαυτοῦ ἐν ἀγάπῃ
verses.king_james: From whom the whole body fitly joined together and compacted by that which every joint supplieth, according to the effectual working in the measure of every part, maketh increase of the body unto the edifying of itself in love.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc igitur dico, et testificor in Domino, ut jam non ambuletis, sicut et gentes ambulant in vanitate sensus sui,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο οὖν λέγω καὶ μαρτύρομαι ἐν κυρίῳ μηκέτι ὑμᾶς περιπατεῖν καθὼς καὶ τὰ λοιπὰ ἔθνη περιπατεῖ ἐν ματαιότητι τοῦ νοὸς αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that ye henceforth walk not as other Gentiles walk, in the vanity of their mind,

verses.latin_vulgate: tenebris obscuratum habentes intellectum, alienati a vita Dei per ignorantiam, quæ est in illis, propter cæcitatem cordis ipsorum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐσκοτισμένοι τῇ διανοίᾳ ὄντες ἀπηλλοτριωμένοι τῆς ζωῆς τοῦ θεοῦ διὰ τὴν ἄγνοιαν τὴν οὖσαν ἐν αὐτοῖς διὰ τὴν πώρωσιν τῆς καρδίας αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Having the understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God through the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui desperantes, semetipsos tradiderunt impudicitiæ, in operationem immunditiæ omnis in avaritiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἵτινες ἀπηλγηκότες ἑαυτοὺς παρέδωκαν τῇ ἀσελγείᾳ εἰς ἐργασίαν ἀκαθαρσίας πάσης ἐν πλεονεξίᾳ
verses.king_james: Who being past feeling have given themselves over unto lasciviousness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos autem non ita didicistis Christum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ οὐχ οὕτως ἐμάθετε τὸν χριστόν
verses.king_james: But ye have not so learned Christ;

verses.latin_vulgate: si tamen illum audistis, et in ipso edocti estis, sicut est veritas in Jesu,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἴγε αὐτὸν ἠκούσατε καὶ ἐν αὐτῷ ἐδιδάχθητε καθώς ἐστιν ἀλήθεια ἐν τῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: If so be that ye have heard him, and have been taught by him, as the truth is in Jesus:

verses.latin_vulgate: deponere vos secundum pristinam conversationem veterem hominem, qui corrumpitur secundum desideria erroris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποθέσθαι ὑμᾶς κατὰ τὴν προτέραν ἀναστροφὴν τὸν παλαιὸν ἄνθρωπον τὸν φθειρόμενον κατὰ τὰς ἐπιθυμίας τῆς ἀπάτης
verses.king_james: That ye put off concerning the former conversation the old man, which is corrupt according to the deceitful lusts;

verses.latin_vulgate: Renovamini autem spiritu mentis vestræ,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνανεοῦσθαι δὲ τῷ πνεύματι τοῦ νοὸς ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And be renewed in the spirit of your mind;

verses.latin_vulgate: et induite novum hominem, qui secundum Deum creatus est in justitia, et sanctitate veritatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐνδύσασθαι τὸν καινὸν ἄνθρωπον τὸν κατὰ θεὸν κτισθέντα ἐν δικαιοσύνῃ καὶ ὁσιότητι τῆς ἀληθείας
verses.king_james: And that ye put on the new man, which after God is created in righteousness and true holiness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod deponentes mendacium, loquimini veritatem unusquisque cum proximo suo: quoniam sumus invicem membra.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ ἀποθέμενοι τὸ ψεῦδος λαλεῖτε ἀλήθειαν ἕκαστος μετὰ τοῦ πλησίον αὐτοῦ ὅτι ἐσμὲν ἀλλήλων μέλη
verses.king_james: Wherefore putting away lying, speak every man truth with his neighbour: for we are members one of another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Irascimini, et nolite peccare: sol non occidat super iracundiam vestram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀργίζεσθε καὶ μὴ ἁμαρτάνετε ὁ ἥλιος μὴ ἐπιδυέτω ἐπὶ τῷ παροργισμῷ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: Be ye angry, and sin not: let not the sun go down upon your wrath:

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite locum dare diabolo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μήτε δίδοτε τόπον τῷ διαβόλῳ
verses.king_james: Neither give place to the devil.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui furabatur, jam non furetur: magis autem laboret, operando manibus suis, quod bonum est, ut habeat unde tribuat necessitatem patienti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ κλέπτων μηκέτι κλεπτέτω μᾶλλον δὲ κοπιάτω ἐργαζόμενος τὸ ἀγαθόν ταῖς χερσὶν ἵνα ἔχῃ μεταδιδόναι τῷ χρείαν ἔχοντι
verses.king_james: Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with *his* hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis sermo malus ex ore vestro non procedat: sed si quis bonus ad ædificationem fidei ut det gratiam audientibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶς λόγος σαπρὸς ἐκ τοῦ στόματος ὑμῶν μὴ ἐκπορευέσθω ἀλλ᾽ εἴ τις ἀγαθὸς πρὸς οἰκοδομὴν τῆς χρείας ἵνα δῷ χάριν τοῖς ἀκούουσιν
verses.king_james: Let no corrupt communication proceed out of your mouth, but that which is good to the use of edifying, that it may minister grace unto the hearers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nolite contristare Spiritum Sanctum Dei: in quo signati estis in diem redemptionis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ λυπεῖτε τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον τοῦ θεοῦ ἐν ᾧ ἐσφραγίσθητε εἰς ἡμέραν ἀπολυτρώσεως
verses.king_james: And grieve not the holy Spirit of God, whereby ye are sealed unto the day of redemption.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnis amaritudo, et ira, et indignatio, et clamor, et blasphemia tollatur a vobis cum omni malitia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πᾶσα πικρία καὶ θυμὸς καὶ ὀργὴ καὶ κραυγὴ καὶ βλασφημία ἀρθήτω ἀφ᾽ ὑμῶν σὺν πάσῃ κακίᾳ
verses.king_james: Let all bitterness, and wrath, and anger, and clamour, and evil speaking, be put away from you, with all malice:

verses.latin_vulgate: Estote autem invicem benigni, misericordes, donantes invicem sicut et Deus in Christo donavit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γίνεσθε δὲ εἰς ἀλλήλους χρηστοί εὔσπλαγχνοι χαριζόμενοι ἑαυτοῖς καθὼς καὶ ὁ θεὸς ἐν χριστῷ ἐχαρίσατο ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: And be ye kind one to another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God for Christ's sake hath forgiven you.
